reasoning: There is no FDA-approved or globally recognized vaccine for Helicobacter pylori, despite a Phase 3 trial in China completed in 2015. Reviews published as recently as 2024 and 2025 confirm that developing a qualified H. pylori vaccine remains an "ongoing pursuit." Since vaccine development and approval timelines span years, it is highly improbable a new vaccine will be developed and officially recognized by February 2026. Consequently, Wikipedia editors following strict medical sourcing guidelines (WP:MEDRS) are very likely to keep the vaccine status as "No" or blank (which resolves as No). The primary path to a "Yes" resolution would be an aggressive Wikipedia edit citing the 2015 Chinese trial that somehow remains stable on the exact resolution dates, which is unlikely but slightly increases in probability over longer time horizons due to general uncertainty.
